step.title: Scaffold Command
step.goal: Create the command directory and initial template files. This establishes the foundation that will be filled incrementally.

substep.title: Determine Location
substep.action:
Command source lives at a flat path — agent scoping is the config's `destination` field, not a nested folder:
- .vera/commands/{{command_name}}/
For an agent-specific command the directory is the same; only `destination` (and the instructions.md frontmatter `agent:`) changes.

substep.title: Create Directory Structure
substep.output: Created command scaffold: - command-config.yaml (build descriptor template) - instructions.md (frontmatter + body template)

substep.title: Verify Scaffold
substep.action: Confirm the scaffold was created correctly: - Directory exists at .vera/commands/{{command_name}}/ - command-config.yaml has the build descriptor (type/id/file_name/destination/includes) - instructions.md has frontmatter + body template
substep.template-output:
## Command Scaffold Created
**Location:** {{command_location}}
**Files:** - command-config.yaml (build descriptor) - instructions.md (ready for identity + instructions)
[c] Continue to configure identity

section.title: Directory Structure
section.description: Command source files follow a flat structure under .vera/commands/.
section.structure: .vera/commands/ +-- command-name/ # one dir per command (global OR agent-specific) +-- command-config.yaml # type/id/file_name/destination/includes +-- instructions.md # frontmatter (description, ...) + body
section.note: Agent scoping is the config's `destination` field — "." for a global command, or the agent name for an agent-specific one — NOT a nested agent folder. Register the command in .vera/vera.manifest.yaml so it builds.

section.title: Template Files
section.description: Initial templates provide structure to fill in. The command's IDENTITY (description, argument-hint, agent) lives in the instructions.md frontmatter; command-config.yaml is purely the vera build descriptor.
section.template: type: command id: {{command_name}} file_name: {{command_name}}.md destination: "." # "." = global; an agent name = agent-specific includes: - instructions.md

--- description: TODO - describe what this command does argument-hint: "[args]" # agent: {{owner_agent}} # only for agent-specific commands ---
<command name="{{command_name}}"> <instructions> TODO: Add instructions </instructions>
<rules> <rule>TODO: Add rules</rule> </rules> </command>

--- description: TODO - describe what this command does ---
<command name="{{command_name}}"> <instructions> <action>Use the {{target_skill}} skill</action> <action>Pass $ARGUMENTS as context</action> </instructions> </command>
